incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 (severity 9.1) in FortiClient EMS, with in-the-wild attacks ramping up over the Easter weekend.
incidents.impact: Federal agencies given days to patch; second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks increases defender fatigue. Widely used across governments globally.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a wiper payload through its existing cloud worm infrastructure (CanisterWorm), targeting systems with Iran's timezone or Farsi language settings.
incidents.impact: If Kubernetes clusters are detected, data is destroyed on every node; otherwise the local machine is wiped. Opportunistic injection into Iran conflict by a criminal group.

incidents.vector: Wiper attack by MOIS-affiliated hacktivist group claiming to have erased data from over 200,000 systems, servers, and mobile devices across 79 countries.
incidents.impact: Over 5,000 workers sent home in Ireland; order processing, manufacturing, and shipping disrupted for three weeks. Company now says it is fully operational. Claimed as retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

incidents.vector: Use-after-free vulnerability in Chrome's Dawn WebGPU implementation, the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026.
incidents.impact: CISA added to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Google released emergency patch. Fourth actively exploited Chrome zero-day this year signals sustained targeting of browser attack surface.

incidents.vector: North Korean hackers socially engineered the lead Axios maintainer via a fake company video call, installed malware, and published poisoned npm packages containing a remote access trojan.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions were live for several hours, potentially reaching ~600,000 downloads. Embedded RAT stole credentials including AWS keys, GitHub tokens, and SSH keys. Downstream impacts still unfolding. Google attributed the attack to UNC1069.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055 (severity 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler, enabling unauthenticated memory read of sensitive data — reminiscent of prior CitrixBleed vulnerabilities.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered emergency patching. Exploitation confirmed by watchTowr. Widely deployed in enterprise environments for authentication and VPN; hospitals and critical infrastructure at particular risk.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access discovered March 28; some systems taken offline while business continuity plans activated.
incidents.impact: 8-K filed with SEC. Company continues to take orders and ship product but warns full resolution may take several weeks. Indicators consistent with ransomware.

incidents.vector: Previously disclosed DoS vulnerability CVE-2025-53521 in F5 BIG-IP APM upgraded to critical unauthenticated RCE, with active exploitation confirmed.
incidents.impact: NCSC and CISA issued urgent advisories. F5 recommends isolating affected systems and investigating for compromise. Common in large enterprises for access management.

incidents.vector: Attackers breached the Commission's AWS account via a compromised API key gained through the Trivy supply chain attack, exfiltrating 92 GB of data.
incidents.impact: Data from 42 internal clients and 29 EU entities potentially stolen, including ~52,000 outbound email files. Stolen data appeared on ShinyHunters dark web site on March 28. CERT-EU attributed the breach to TeamPCP with high confidence.

incidents.vector: Multi-stage supply chain campaign starting with Trivy compromise, spreading to Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, Telnyx, and npm/Docker packages. Credential-stealing malware harvested ~300 GB of secrets from CI/CD pipelines.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments impacted per Mandiant. Downstream victims expected to grow to 10,000+. TeamPCP now operating dual ransomware tracks (CipherForce and Vect) and collaborating with LAPSUS$. CISA KEV deadline April 8.

incidents.vector: LAPSUS$ claimed a 3 GB data breach of AstraZeneca, releasing the archive for free after failing to find buyers. Likely linked to TeamPCP credential trove.
incidents.impact: Partially verified dump includes GitHub developer data, employee records from clinical research subsidiaries, and internal source code structures. AstraZeneca has not confirmed or denied at 96+ hours, raising GDPR exposure. Potential HIPAA and PHI implications.

incidents.vector: Qilin ransomware group claimed responsibility for an attack at the end of March that forced the party to shut down its IT network.
incidents.impact: Employee records stolen; party says no member data was compromised. IT network shut down for containment.

incidents.vector: Russia-based actors targeting messaging apps (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger) to compromise accounts of government officials through phishing, device-linking tricks, and QR code lures.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint advisory. Attackers attempt to add devices to accounts, join group chats undetected, and impersonate known contacts. Follows prior FSB targeting campaigns.

incidents.victim: Diplomatic organizations and critical infrastructure (Middle East)
incidents.vector: MOIS-affiliated group conducting cyberespionage using new Rust-based malware (LampoRAT, BlackBeard) and UDP-based backdoors with AI-assisted development techniques.
incidents.impact: Active campaigns targeting diplomatic, energy, maritime, and financial organizations across the Middle East. Shift to Rust likely aimed at evading defenses and achieving longer-term persistence.

incidents.vector: Chinese hackers exploited a vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads to dozens of government agencies via a compromised on-premises server.
incidents.impact: CISA gave agencies two weeks to patch. Campaign used Havoc pentesting tool and ShadowPad malware. TrueConf serves ~100,000 organizations, primarily government, military, and critical infrastructure sectors.

incidents.vector: Large-scale automated credential harvesting exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js apps via a custom framework called NEXUS Listener.
incidents.impact: Attackers automatically extract cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys from hundreds of compromised hosts. Became the top-targeted vulnerability of 2025 despite late disclosure. Creates risks for lateral movement and supply chain integrity.

incidents.vector: Threat actor used an AI coding agent with compromised credentials to bypass security measures and upload a backdoored update package to the BuddyBoss platform.
incidents.impact: Nearly 250 websites compromised, mostly American universities located in the Middle East. Researchers tracked the attack to a French threat actor. Notable for AI-assisted supply chain attack methodology.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners (3M+ devices); US DoD
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised 3M+ IoT devices including routers and cameras, launching hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks including against DoD addresses.
incidents.impact: DOJ and international partners seized domains and infrastructure. Aisuru alone issued 200,000+ attack commands. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Infrastructure dismantled to prevent further infections.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to internal information-sharing system detected February 28; customer, partner, and employee personal data confirmed stolen.
incidents.impact: Core manufacturing and sales systems unaffected. Unauthorized access path has been blocked and external access restrictions strengthened. No evidence of malware upload to internal systems.

incidents.vector: Alleged compromise linked to TeamPCP's credential harvest from the Trivy supply chain attack. Screenshots showing AWS artifacts and STS tokens match TeamPCP's playbook.
incidents.impact: Databricks says it has investigated and found nothing in internal systems but is requesting more information. If confirmed, would be the first major cloud platform identified as a downstream victim of TeamPCP's credential trove. Investigation ongoing.

incidents.vector: Security breach of customer support portal, with attackers believed to have stolen personal data from support tickets.
incidents.impact: Personal data shared in support tickets compromised. Company sells weight-loss drugs and sexual health prescriptions, making the data especially sensitive.
